ticket http://projects.scipy.org/scipy/ticket/950 on 2009-05-24 by trac user dmitrey.kroshko, assigned to unknown._

BOBYQA is optimization solver for box-bounded NLP/NSP problems, when no derivatives are available. One says currently it's state-of-the-art solver for the problems mentioned.

License is BSD-compatible. 

More info is [http://forum.openopt.org/viewtopic.php?id=20 here]. 

Fortran source is [http://plato.asu.edu/ftp/other_software/bobyqa.zip here].

Enhancement","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null},{"id":16917029,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNjkxNzAyOQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/component:%20numpy.core","name":"component: numpy.core","color":"009800","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":10,"created_at":"2017-03-21T08:05:20Z","updated_at":"2022-07-16T18:59:04Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"It would be useful if NumPy had a modular exponentiation function.  Besides performance improvements over `a**b%m`, it also allows computations where `a**b` would not fit in the datatype.

NumPy also seems to ignore Python's third argument to `pow` (the modulus), rather than raising an error if it's set.

    In [2]: pow(np.arange(10), 10, 3)
    Out[2]: 
    array([         0,          1,       1024,      59049,    1048576,
              9765625,   60466176,  282475249, 1073741824, 3486784401])","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/8804/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":4,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/8804/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1186266486","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/8804#issuecomment-1186266486","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/8804","id":1186266486,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP85GtP12","user":{"login":"lorepieri8","id":25624916,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1NjI0OTE2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25624916?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8","html_url":"https://github.com/lorepieri8","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-07-16T18:59:04Z","updated_at":"2022-07-16T18:59:04Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Any plans to see this live? {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383/events","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/23383","id":1207492359,"node_id":"I_kwDOAAnGFs5H-N8H","number":23383,"title":"Sympy subs not outputting a substituted expression","user":{"login":"lorepieri8","id":25624916,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1NjI0OTE2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25624916?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8","html_url":"https://github.com/lorepieri8","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-04-18T21:57:44Z","updated_at":"2022-04-20T17:25:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`.evalf(subs={"k":1})`

has no substitution effect on some expressions. Full example on this SO post:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61465322/sympy-subs-not-outputting-a-substituted-expression/66065695#66065695

Tested on Sympy 1.10.1 

Example:


```
from sympy import *

x, t = symbols('x t')

def expt(m):
    return exp(t * (x - x ** m) / (1 - x ** m))

m = 2
s = series(expt(m), x)
ls = Add.make_args(s)
ls2 = [l.evalf(subs={x:1}) for l in ls]
print(ls2[1])

>>> t*x
```



(instead of the correct result, t )","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/1104213982","html_url":"https://github.com/sympy/sympy/issues/23383#issuecomment-1104213982","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/23383","id":1104213982,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAAnGFs5B0Pfe","user":{"login":"lorepieri8","id":25624916,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1NjI0OTE2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25624916?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8","html_url":"https://github.com/lorepieri8","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lorepieri8/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-04-20T17:25:03Z","updated_at":"2022-04-20T17:25:03Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"The problem can be circumvented by setting x=1.0 instead of x=1.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sympy/sympy/issues/comments/1104213982/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
